skeletons.template:
.section .data
hello_msg:
    .ascii "Hello, GAS!\n"
    len = . - hello_msg

.section .text
.global _start

_start:
    # sys_write syscall
    mov $1, %rax        # syscall number for write
    mov $1, %rdi        # file descriptor 1 (stdout)
    lea hello_msg(%rip), %rsi # message address
    mov $len, %rdx      # message length
    syscall

    # sys_exit syscall
    mov $60, %rax       # syscall number for exit
    xor %rdi, %rdi      # exit code 0
    syscall

# A function that adds two numbers
# Input: %rdi = first number, %rsi = second number
# Output: %rax = sum

.section .text
.global _start
.global add_numbers

_start:
    # Example of calling the function
    mov $10, %rdi
    mov $32, %rsi
    call add_numbers

    # %rax now holds 42
    # Exit with the result as the status code
    mov $60, %rax
    mov %rax, %rdi
    syscall

add_numbers:
    push %rbp           # Save the base pointer
    mov %rsp, %rbp      # Set up the new stack frame

    mov %rdi, %rax      # Move the first argument into rax
    add %rsi, %rax      # Add the second argument to rax

    mov %rbp, %rsp      # Restore the stack pointer
    pop %rbp            # Restore the base pointer
    ret

.section .text
.global _start

_start:
    mov $5, %rcx        # Set loop counter to 5
    mov $0, %rax        # Initialize accumulator

loop_start:
    add %rcx, %rax      # Add current counter value to rax
    dec %rcx            # Decrement counter
    jnz loop_start      # If counter is not zero, jump to loop_start

    # After loop, rax = 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15

    # Exit with the result
    mov $60, %rax
    mov %rax, %rdi
    syscall

.section .bss
    .lcomm buffer, 100 # Reserve 100 bytes for input

.section .data
    prompt_msg: .ascii "Enter your name: "
    prompt_len = . - prompt_msg

.section .text
.global _start

_start:
    # Print prompt
    mov $1, %rax
    mov $1, %rdi
    lea prompt_msg(%rip), %rsi
    mov $prompt_len, %rdx
    syscall

    # sys_read syscall
    mov $0, %rax                # syscall number for read
    mov $0, %rdi                # file descriptor 0 (stdin)
    lea buffer(%rip), %rsi      # buffer to store input
    mov $100, %rdx              # max bytes to read
    syscall

    # rax now contains number of bytes read
    # Print the input back to the user
    mov $1, %rax
    mov $1, %rdi
    lea buffer(%rip), %rsi
    mov %rax, %rdx # Use the length returned by sys_read
    syscall

    # Exit
    mov $60, %rax
    xor %rdi, %rdi
    syscall

.section .data
    format_str: .asciz "The number is %d\n"

.section .text
.global main
.extern printf

main:
    push %rbp
    mov %rsp, %rbp

    # Call printf(format_str, 123)
    lea format_str(%rip), %rdi  # First argument (format string)
    mov $123, %rsi              # Second argument (the number)
    mov $0, %rax                # No vector registers used
    call printf

    mov $0, %rax
    leave
    ret
